$comment: Descriptor for each SignalOps variant: where its source lives, what to measure, and how to attribute files. Shared 'socle' packages (contracts, fixtures, metrics, ui-spec, test-scenarios) are intentionally NOT attributed to any single variant so the comparison reflects variant-specific engineering. The `ci` block is the abstraction layer for the variant-level CI matrix (pass 2.5): per-variant build/typecheck/lint/test commands + Docker descriptor, so the heterogeneous toolchains (nx, pnpm filters, cargo/next) collect into one comparable JSON shape.

changeExperiment.$comment: The controlled cost-of-change experiment (docs/product/03-ai-task-protocol.md): the SAME product intent — the Risk trend capability — implemented in all three variants against the same spec (derive from riskScore: >=80 up, <=35 down, else stable; /signals column with a text badge; filter; detail view; tests). The collector measures each variant's REAL feature footprint from these identifiers; no expected number exists anywhere.

variants.label: Flow
variants.thesis: The balanced choice — modular, readable, and the cheapest to change.

ci.$comment_commands: Each variant's commands are ITS OWN full validation protocol over ITS ENTIRE own code, mirroring its real CI gate. Flow = every scope:flow project (app + 15 packages), like flow-ci's run-many (scope:shared socle excluded for all variants).
ci.$comment_warm: Warm re-validation = the SAME gates re-run right after the cold pass, with the variant's real cache strategy in play. For Flow that is Nx computation caching (the cold pass disables it with --skip-nx-cache, so warm must drop the flag). Friction/Overfit have no warmCommands override: their cold commands ARE their everyday commands, so the warm pass re-runs them as-is and measures whatever incremental behaviour their toolchains really have.

feedback.$comment: Real cached CI feedback source: the quality job(s) of flow-ci.yml on GitHub Actions. Excludes the advisory a11y job.

variants.label: Friction
variants.thesis: Locally fast, globally expensive — fewer parts, tangled and harder to trust.

docker.$comment: Friction ships two images (web + api); we build the user-facing web image for a comparable single-image size/startup signal. The api image is noted but not the scored artifact.

feedback.$comment: Real cached CI feedback source: the single quality job of friction-ci.yml on GitHub Actions.

variants.label: Overfit
variants.thesis: Technically impressive, dangerously expensive — over-engineered past its payoff.

ci.$comment: Overfit is deliberately polyglot, and its own CI gate (ci:overfit) validates BOTH toolchains: TS/Next AND the Rust workspace (fmt/clippy/test/release build). Excluding cargo would hide the variant's real validation burden, so each step chains its TS gate with its Rust twin. Test counts are parsed from the combined vitest + cargo output.

docker.$comment: Overfit ships web (Next.js) + api (Rust) images; we build the user-facing web image for a comparable single-image signal.

feedback.$comment: Real cached CI feedback source: the quality job (gate) of overfit-ci.yml on GitHub Actions. Excludes the warm-cache priming job.
